config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum bagi tiap orang. Kalau prioritasnya terlalu berat ke masyarakat, mudah sekali muncul pembenaran untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should automatically come before individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the greater good" can be used to justify abuse. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and limits on government power. If individual rights are treated as optional, minorities and unpopular people are usually the first to lose protection. In my view, a healthy society is one that protects individuals first enough to prevent collective goals from becoming a cover for overreach.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling rentan biasanya kelompok kecil atau orang yang suaranya tidak populer. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder, negara dan mayoritas jadi terlalu mudah mengklaim bahwa penindasan itu demi kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society's interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse overreach. Yes, public safety, public health, and social order matter, but they are only legitimate when they are constrained by rights, due process, and clear limits on power. The point is not that the individual always wins, but that society should not get a blank check to override people just because the majority says it is for the common good. When rights become secondary, unpopular groups are usually the first ones harmed, and that is exactly what a free society should guard against.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih tinggi daripada hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling sering kena dampaknya justru kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya suara kuat. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, proses yang adil, dan perlindungan terhadap penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kalau hak individu dibuat nomor dua, masyarakat malah membuka pintu bagi mayoritas atau negara untuk menekan yang berbeda atas nama kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that kind of argument has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach under the banner of “the common good.” Society does matter, but it is strongest when it protects basic rights first and only limits them in narrow, clearly justified cases with due process. If we make individual rights secondary, the people most likely to suffer are the ones with the least power and the least popular views.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
